proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90, mean 65) is positively associated with pfs_months across the full cohort.
feature_005 (ordinal, levels 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: increasing levels correspond to shorter PFS.
feature_006 (binary, 30% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months than feature_006=0.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.702 between feature_012 and pfs_months (p<1e-300). feature_012 is the single strongest individual predictor of PFS in the cohort.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.f_oneway over feature_005 levels
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_005: level 0 = 5.97 mo, level 1 = 4.43 mo, level 2 = 3.62 mo. Top-vs-bottom mean difference = -2.35 mo (one-way ANOVA F=2861, p<1e-300).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_006
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_006=1: 3.62 mo (n=14969); feature_006=0: 5.15 mo (n=35031). Mean difference = -1.54 mo, Welch t=-66.6, p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (binary, 10% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_013 (binary, 35% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_013=0.
feature_029 (binary, 70% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months than feature_029=0.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_001=1: 3.75 mo (n=5090); feature_001=0: 4.80 mo (n=44910). Mean difference = -1.05 mo, p<1e-100.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_013
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_013=1: 5.41 mo (n=17428); feature_013=0: 4.31 mo (n=32572). Mean difference = +1.10 mo, p<1e-300.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_029
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_029=1: 4.86 mo (n=34886); feature_029=0: 4.30 mo (n=15114). Mean difference = +0.57 mo, p~3.7e-143.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (binary, 18% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_028 (binary, 35% prevalence) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_023 (binary, 64% prevalence) is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_018
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_018=1: 4.32 mo (n=9060); feature_018=0: 4.78 mo (n=40940). Mean difference = -0.46 mo, p~6.1e-70.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_028=1: 4.33 mo (n=17365); feature_028=0: 4.89 mo (n=32635). Mean difference = -0.56 mo, p~6.1e-144.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on pfs_months by feature_023
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS feature_023=1: 4.82 mo (n=31954); feature_023=0: 4.46 mo (n=18046). Mean difference = +0.36 mo, p~5.8e-59.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_033 (continuous, range 1-100, mean 15.5) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_024 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean 3.8) is positively correlated with pfs_months.
feature_004 (continuous, range 0-24.6, mean 3.84) is negatively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_033'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.091 (p ~3e-93). Modest negative correlation; effect-size scale appears small at the marginal level but feature_033 may be a strong modifier (probed later).

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_024'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.100 (p ~9e-112). feature_024 is positively but modestly correlated with PFS.

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr(df['feature_004'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = -0.116 (p ~4e-148). Negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable OLS regression, feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_001, feature_013, feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, feature_033, feature_024, and feature_004 each retain independent associations with pfs_months in their previously observed directions.

Most other features (e.g., feature_011, feature_019, feature_030, feature_016, feature_021, feature_003, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_027, feature_034, feature_014, feature_037, feature_017, feature_035, feature_022, feature_008, feature_032, feature_020, feature_025, feature_036, feature_015, feature_009, feature_026) lose statistical significance once the strong predictors are adjusted for.

analyses.code: statsmodels OLS pfs_months ~ all 37 features
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.792. Independent coefficients (months PFS / unit): feature_012 +0.176, feature_005 -1.17, feature_006 -1.55, feature_001 -0.98, feature_013 +1.10, feature_029 +0.53, feature_028 -0.58, feature_018 -0.47, feature_033 -0.019, feature_024 +0.48, feature_004 -0.077, feature_031 -0.0005. All p<1e-15.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS coefficient table
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, feature_011, feature_019, feature_030, feature_016, feature_023, feature_026, feature_036, feature_021, feature_003, feature_015, feature_022, feature_008, feature_020, feature_025, feature_034, feature_014, feature_037, feature_017, feature_035, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_027 are individually non-significant (p>0.05). Note: feature_023's univariate signal is largely captured by feature_013 / feature_029.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013, the binary feature with the largest positive multivariable coefficient and biologically plausible prevalence (~35%), behaves like a treatment whose effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous across patient subgroups (rather than uniform).

analyses.code: OLS interaction screen: pfs_months ~ feature_013 * each other feature, plus core covariates
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 has highly significant interactions (all p<<1e-50) with feature_029 (+1.56), feature_023 (+1.03), feature_018 (-1.34), feature_028 (-1.67), and feature_033 (-0.053/unit, continuous), and a smaller interaction with feature_016 (+0.38). These dwarf any feature_013 main effect, supporting strong heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of feature_013 is conditional on feature_029=1: in feature_029=0 patients there is essentially no benefit, while in feature_029=1 patients there is a substantial positive benefit.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference: feature_013 effect within feature_029 strata
analyses.result_summary: feature_029=0: mean PFS feature_013=1 4.29 vs feature_013=0 4.30 -> diff = -0.02 mo (n0=9880, n1=5234, NS). feature_029=1: mean PFS 5.89 vs 4.31 -> diff = +1.58 mo (n0=22692, n1=12194, p<<1e-300). Treatment benefit is essentially absent when feature_029=0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of feature_013 is abolished when feature_018=1: in feature_018=1 patients the treatment effect collapses to zero, whereas it is large and positive when feature_018=0.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference: feature_013 effect within feature_018 strata
analyses.result_summary: feature_018=0: feature_013=1 mean 5.66 vs feature_013=0 4.31 -> diff = +1.35 mo (n0=26727, n1=14213, p<<1e-300). feature_018=1: 4.31 vs 4.32 -> diff = -0.01 mo (n0=5845, n1=3215, NS). feature_018=1 abrogates the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pfs_months benefit of feature_013 is abolished when feature_028=1: in feature_028=1 patients the treatment effect is null, whereas it is large and positive when feature_028=0.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference: feature_013 effect within feature_028 strata
analyses.result_summary: feature_028=0: feature_013=1 mean 5.97 vs feature_013=0 4.31 -> diff = +1.66 mo (n0=21230, n1=11405, p<<1e-300). feature_028=1: 4.35 vs 4.32 -> diff = +0.04 mo (n0=11342, n1=6023, NS). feature_028=1 abrogates the benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_033 attenuates the pfs_months benefit of feature_013 in a continuous, dose-response fashion: each additional unit of feature_033 reduces feature_013's effect by roughly 0.05 months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_033_centered + feature_013:feature_033_centered + covariates
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.053 mo per unit of feature_033 (t=-72.9, p<<1e-300). Treatment effect at feature_033=0 is large; at feature_033=35-40 it is essentially nil (linear extrapolation).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Joint subgroup definition: feature_013 produces a clinically meaningful pfs_months gain only in patients meeting all three of feature_029=1, feature_018=0, and feature_028=0.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference across the 16 cells of feature_029 x feature_023 x feature_018 x feature_028
analyses.result_summary: Among cells with feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0: feature_013 effect is +2.69 mo (f23=0) and +2.95 mo (f23=1), both p<1e-100. In every cell where any of feature_029=0, feature_018=1, or feature_028=1, the feature_013 effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (|diff|<=0.32, all NS).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 'responsive subgroup' defined by feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 contains essentially all of the pfs_months benefit attributable to feature_013; outside this subgroup, feature_013 has no meaningful effect.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_013 within and outside the responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup (n=18,681; 37.4% of cohort): feature_013=1 mean 6.91 vs feature_013=0 4.00 -> diff = +2.91 mo (p<<1e-300). Outside (n=31,319): diff = +0.003 mo (p=0.91, NS). Treatment effect is fully concentrated in the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup (feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0), feature_033 is the strongest residual modifier of feature_013's effect: treatment benefit decreases sharply as feature_033 rises.

analyses.code: Within responsive subgroup, screen each remaining feature for interaction with feature_013 in OLS adjusted for core covariates
analyses.result_summary: feature_033 has the strongest interaction within responsive subgroup (coef = -0.138 mo / unit, p~0). All other interactions are weak (best second p~0.005, coef magnitude <0.2). No other binary feature qualitatively flips the effect within the subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive subgroup, feature_013 benefit is largest when feature_033 is in the lowest tertile (~1-9), intermediate in the middle tertile (9-16.5), and essentially zero in the highest tertile (>=16.5).

analyses.code: Tertile-stratified mean-difference within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup: feature_033 low tertile (1.0-8.9) feature_013 effect = +4.94 mo (n=6256); mid tertile (9.0-16.5) +3.69 mo (n=6253); high tertile (>=16.6) -0.02 mo (n=6172). Steep dose-response by feature_033 with effective null in the high tertile.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A threshold of feature_033<=15 within the responsive subgroup captures essentially the entire feature_013 benefit; above feature_033>15 within the same subgroup, the benefit is null.

analyses.code: Threshold sweep on feature_033 within responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup, feature_013 effect by threshold: <=10 +4.96 mo / >10 +1.57 mo; <=12 +4.96 / >12 +0.91; <=15 +4.65 / >15 +0.02; <=18 +4.09 / >18 -0.06. The cleanest cut is feature_033<=15 where effect above threshold is essentially zero.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Refined treatment-effect subgroup: feature_013 produces ~+5 months of pfs_months benefit in patients meeting all four predicates feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=15; the benefit is essentially absent (close to 0 mo, NS) in any patient violating any one of these predicates.

analyses.code: Welch t-test of pfs_months by feature_013 within full 4-predicate subgroup vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup (n=11,575, 23.2% of cohort): feature_013=1 mean ~9.0 vs feature_013=0 mean ~4.3 -> diff = +4.65 mo, p<<1e-300. Outside (n=38,425): diff <= +0.4 mo overall and 0.0 in any cell that violates only one of the four predicates. The four-predicate definition is the tightest plausible heterogeneity boundary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_023 (positive univariate effect on PFS) is largely a proxy for the treatment-responsive subgroup membership and does not add meaningful independent treatment-effect modification once feature_029 / feature_018 / feature_028 / feature_033 are conditioned on.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference by feature_023 within the full responsive subgroup (29=1,18=0,28=0,33<=15)
analyses.result_summary: Inside responsive subgroup: feature_023=0 feature_013 effect = +4.33 mo (n0=1118,n1=593); feature_023=1 effect = +4.71 mo (n0=6359,n1=3505). Difference between f023 strata is small (~0.4 mo) compared with the overall +4.7 mo effect, indicating feature_023 is at most a weak refinement.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_016 modestly enhances feature_013 effect in the full-cohort interaction screen, but this is not sustained as an independent modifier inside the responsive subgroup once the four-predicate definition is applied.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference by feature_016 within and outside the responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responsive subgroup: f016=0 feature_013 effect +2.90 mo, f016=1 effect +2.92 mo (both halves yield the same effect). The full-cohort feature_016 interaction (+0.38) is mostly driven by feature_016 correlating with subgroup membership rather than independently modifying the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pure (unmodified) main effect of feature_013, holding everything else equal, in patients without any of the responsive features, is essentially zero, not the +1.10 mo seen in the marginal univariate comparison.

analyses.code: OLS with full feature_013 x {feature_029, feature_018, feature_028, feature_033_centered} interactions plus covariates; main feature_013 coefficient at the reference (feature_029=0, feature_018=0, feature_028=0, feature_033=mean)
analyses.result_summary: In the joint-interaction OLS, the feature_013 main coefficient at the reference level (with feature_033 centered at the cohort mean of 15.5) is +0.85 mo, but most of this represents 'partial responsive' patients who still have feature_029=0; once we additionally condition on feature_029=0 directly, the feature_013 mean effect is -0.02 mo (unadjusted). The marginal +1.10 univariate effect arises because subgroup-membership variables are correlated with feature_013 prevalence and outcomes; the within-stratum effect among non-responders is null.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005 (ordinal, levels 0-2) is a generic prognostic marker that depresses pfs_months uniformly across feature_013 strata (no interaction with treatment).
feature_006 (binary), feature_001 (binary), feature_004 (continuous), and feature_031 (continuous) are generic negative prognostic markers without treatment-effect modification.

analyses.code: Stratified mean-difference: feature_013 effect within levels of feature_005, restricted to responsive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Inside responsive subgroup (29=1,18=0,28=0): feature_013 effect +2.92 (f005=0), +2.92 (f005=1), +2.97 (f005=2). Effect is constant across levels; feature_005 is purely prognostic, not a modifier.

analyses.code: Pairwise OLS interaction tests of feature_013 with feature_006, feature_001, feature_004, feature_031 (cohort-wide)
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 x feature_013 interaction p=0.65 (NS); feature_001 x feature_013 p=0.22 (NS); feature_004 x feature_013 p=0.31 (NS); feature_031 x feature_013 p=0.17 (NS). All four behave as additive main effects without modifying feature_013's effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is the only binary feature in the cohort with a clear treatment-effect heterogeneity profile: no other binary feature shows comparable interaction structure when systematically tested as a candidate treatment.

analyses.code: Systematic screen: for each binary feature_X, test pfs_months ~ feature_X * feature_Y (over all Y) and report the smallest interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top results: feature_013 x feature_028 (p=4e-255, coef=-1.62); feature_029 x feature_013 (p=9e-229, +1.59); feature_018 x feature_013 (p=2e-118, -1.36); feature_023 x feature_013 (p=3e-117, +1.09); feature_016 x feature_013 (p=9e-19, +0.42). All top entries involve feature_013 with the four predicates already identified. The next strongest non-feature_013 candidate is feature_006 x feature_012 (p=3e-12, coef=-0.011/yr), and represents only continuous-modifier dampening of a prognostic effect, not a treatment story. No alternative treatment candidate emerges.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012, the strongest single predictor of pfs_months, is a generic prognostic factor (not a treatment-effect modifier of feature_013), since its association with PFS is similar in feature_013=1 and feature_013=0 patients.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_012 + feature_013:feature_012 + core covariates
analyses.result_summary: feature_013:feature_012 interaction is small (|coef|<0.005 mo per unit, p>0.05 once subgroup variables are controlled). The +0.176 mo/unit main effect of feature_012 holds across feature_013 strata. feature_012 acts as prognostic, not predictive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the four-predicate responsive subgroup, the magnitude of the feature_013 benefit is large enough that treating feature_013 as a treatment with a near-binary biomarker logic (responsive/non-responsive) explains substantially more variance than treating feature_013 as a uniform main effect plus feature_033 alone.

analyses.code: Compare R^2 of (a) OLS with feature_013 main effect only + covariates; (b) OLS adding feature_013 x {feature_029, feature_018, feature_028, feature_033_centered} interactions
analyses.result_summary: Model (a) R^2=0.792. Model (b) R^2=0.858. Adding the four feature_013 interactions explains an additional 6.6 percentage points of variance (~33% of residual variance after model a), confirming the heterogeneity is a major component of the outcome model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous markers feature_021, feature_003, feature_002, feature_007, feature_010, feature_017, feature_034, feature_014, feature_037, feature_035, feature_027 do not show treatment-effect heterogeneity with feature_013 once the four-predicate subgroup is conditioned on.

analyses.code: Within responsive subgroup, OLS pfs_months ~ feature_013 + feature_X + feature_013:feature_X for each continuous X (with covariates)
analyses.result_summary: All listed continuous markers have within-subgroup interaction p>=0.005 and small coefficients (<0.2 mo per unit). After Bonferroni correction across 11 tests (alpha=0.0045), none survives. They behave as marginal prognostic noise rather than additional modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis: feature_013 (treatment) increases pfs_months by approximately +4.7 months relative to feature_013=0, but only in the subgroup defined by feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=15. In any patient where any one of these four predicates is violated, the feature_013 effect on pfs_months is statistically indistinguishable from zero. This is the single most parsimonious heterogeneity description supported by the cohort.

Companion main-effect summary: feature_012 (positive, +0.176 mo/unit), feature_005 (negative, -1.17 mo/unit), feature_006 (negative, -1.55 mo), feature_001 (negative, -0.98 mo), feature_004 (negative, -0.077 mo/unit), feature_024 (positive, +0.48 mo/unit), feature_033 (negative as a main effect, -0.019 mo/unit on top of its modifier role), and feature_031 (slightly negative, -0.0005 mo/unit) act as cohort-wide prognostic factors that depress or elevate pfs_months independently of feature_013.

analyses.code: Final stratified test: subgroup = (feature_029==1) & (feature_018==0) & (feature_028==0) & (feature_033<=15); compare feature_013=1 vs 0 inside vs outside, plus 4-leave-one-out checks
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup: n=11,575; feature_013=1 mean PFS ~9.0 mo, feature_013=0 mean ~4.3 mo, mean diff = +4.65 mo, Welch p<<1e-300. Outside subgroup: n=38,425; mean diff = +0.42 mo (driven by partial responders with feature_033 between 15 and ~25); cell-level diffs in any cell violating one predicate are 0.0+/-0.3 mo and NS. Leave-one-out: removing each of the four predicates from the subgroup definition diluted the effect (e.g., dropping feature_033<=15 gives +2.91 mo instead of +4.65). The four-predicate definition is the maximal-effect subgroup supported by the data.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS coefficients (recap from iteration 5)
analyses.result_summary: Recap of independent main-effect coefficients in the multivariable OLS used as adjustment set: feature_012 +0.176, feature_005 -1.17, feature_006 -1.55, feature_001 -0.98, feature_024 +0.48, feature_004 -0.077, feature_031 -0.0005, feature_033 -0.019 (main effect, on top of -0.053/unit modifier on feature_013), all p<1e-15.
